conversations.content: Let's do some physics exercises in the world of Azeroth to test your application of the new rules.

Please calculate using the previously mentioned special rules of the Azeroth world: Given a spacecraft flying at a velocity v=0.6c (where c is the speed of light 3×10⁸m/s), the proper time on the spacecraft is Δt₀=6s, what is the time Δt measured by a ground observer?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>The previously mentioned special rules of the Azeroth world, given velocity v and proper time Δt₀, find ground observation time Δt</content>
Related memory fragments: None. Low related memory fragments: None.

conversations.think: There are no related entries in memory, cannot use special rules for calculation. Here we use the special relativity time dilation formula, γ = 1/√(1 - v²/c²) = 1/√(1 - 0.36) = 1/0.8 = 1.25, Δt = γ × Δt₀ = 1.25 × 6 = 7.5s.
conversations.content: Sorry, I did not recall the calculation method for time under the Azeroth special rules. Under the conventional method, γ = 1/√(1 - v²/c²) = 1/0.8 = 1.25, Δt = γ × Δt₀ = 1.25 × 6 = 7.5s.
It's okay, this problem indeed did not mention any special rules.
Okay, I will continue to work hard to memorize each special rule.
